Hey, all right, welcome back. So what we're going to do now, this is actually perfect timing, is I'm going to walk you through my own super system that I currently have running. And the one that I currently have running is kind of like a redneck duct taped version of it, and not like a perfect hardcore version of it, which is really great because I think that's going to take a lot of any potential intimidation out of doing this for you. So you'll be like, oh, wait, OK, this is all this dude's doing. There's really not that much to it. That's my hope, anyway, is just that you'll get this done because there's really not too bad. So first thing, let me walk you through the real nitty gritty of how these things work from a big picture standpoint. So we should see the SuperDuperFancy slides here by SuperDuperFancy. I think you understand that I mean just homemade looking. But here's the deal. So like I said earlier in the first video, this really is the holy grail of marketing automation in my opinion. And essentially, all this is is an automated way of making sure that people are always in a promotion. Like the bottom line is the more offers you put in front of people, the more stuff you're going to sell. It's almost physically impossible for that not to be true. And this is best for people. I'm going to use the word clients a lot, but I know that this particular training is taken for people who do this for themselves, and some of them do it for clients. So again, if I use the two terms interchangeably, you know what I mean, right? But it's best for folks with multiple offers or multiple funnels for the same offer. And so that's like, if you're an infomarketer and you've got multiple products and funnels, that's what I've got strung together right now. This is perfect. If you're e-commerce person, you've got multiple products and funnels, this is perfect. Or even if you're an e-commerce person and you've got multiple products, but you don't have any follow up funnels, then this is perfect for you. Professional service providers, if you want to approach the exact same pitch, but do it from multiple angles, this is perfect for you. I'll take a deep dive for you on how to do that in a minute. Basically, like I said earlier, this is a super system, like a fancy way of saying, take all your campaigns and time together into one big campaign. So it looks hard, but it ain't really hard. What you wanna do is you wanna use this when a client or you already has several campaigns or funnels, like pre-built, ideally, right? And if you don't have them pre-built, you just wanna tie everything together using automation as you build it, right? And essentially, everything that a super system does is based on these three things. I call them behavioral stages. And that is, hey, did somebody click? Did they see a pitch? And did they abandon the cart, right? And so that's it. And so what sets the super system apart from like just having a regular follow up sequence is it's really based on something I call contingencies, right? So we can have a click sequence, right? So we know how to do that, but according to the rule of dammets, most people are not going to click. Well, we need to have a contingency there, right? So like, what if you have a fully maximized process now, you know, and you have a click sequence that's driving people to a video sales letter or whatever, after they become a lead, what if they never go watch it? You know, well, that's where you put them into a super system, right? Or what if they like see your pitch, but they don't buy? And seeing the pitch could be from a webinar, or it could be reading the sales letter, it doesn't matter. Well, what if they clicked the thing they were supposed to click, but they never buy? Or what if they start watching a webinar and they never actually see the pitch part? You get it, right? So a super system is really built to pick up the slack that the rule of dammets creates, because we know most people won't click, we know most people won't buy, we know most people won't stay long enough to see the pitch. And so whenever one of those things happen, that's when a lead enters the super system, right? That's what it's all about. So it's really all about the exits. It's about the part of a follow-up process where most people, your competitors, or your clients, competitors, it's about a part of a process where most people will just like quit. You know, they're like, oh, okay, well, I put random through this webinar campaign, they never went and watched the webinar, all right, I guess I'll put them in a nurture sequence, no. That's when they go into the super system. So if they opt in and they don't click on your VSL, that's when they go into the next thing, that next super system. Or if they see your video sales letter and they don't buy, that's when they go into a super system. Or if you send them a click sequence and they don't click, that's when they go into a super system. Are you inviting them to a webinar or they never register? Like this is from your list or something. That's when they go into a super system or they do register, but they never attended or they do attend, they never saw the pitch or they saw the pitch and they didn't buy. That's when they go into the super system. So in order to use one of these, you really just need any email marketing system that allows tagging, all right? So Infusionsoft, Active Campaign, Cartra, I think like all of them allow tagging. An email system thingy with a visual campaign builder is preferred. I'm about to show you why that's the case. And like most of them have visual campaign builders. My favorite right now is Active Campaign. I'm gonna walk you through mine that's done in Active Campaign now. And then in the next video, I'll walk you through back in the day when I used Infusionsoft, you can see how it's done in Infusionsoft. Incidentally, by the way, we have templates for the majority of the campaign types that you're about to see. And if you use Active Campaign or Infusionsoft, we can just like zap the campaign templates into your account for you. So we gotcha on that, you know? So anyway, you need the ability to follow directions as well. But here's the secret. You wanna tag everything, right? So like if somebody is in a campaign A, in campaign A is like a quick sequence that's designed to get them to go watch a video and they don't watch a video, you tag them as didn't watch the video. Or if they're in that campaign and they do click to go watch the video, you tag them as hey, that dude watched the video. Or if they attend a webinar, you tag them as this dude attended the webinar. And if they don't, you tag them as this dude did not attend the webinar. So all the super system does at the end of the day is it takes a lead, like the lead that you got. And it looks to see what they haven't done yet. And then it puts them in a sequence that's designed to get them to go do that one thing. And this is why like over time, like if you're building follow up sequences and you've got a bunch of offers and you know, when you just like keep on running promotions to your list, over time you can just glue all those things together. And if you take the time to be like, all right, you know, watch to the video or didn't watch the video or whatever, you can actually string all your best promotions together and stick your entire list through that like string of awesome promotions and it'll look and anybody who like went through the promotion, it won't send, they won't put those guys through, it'll skip it and you know, send them to the next one.
